---
title: "Orientation: Antitrust and Regulation"
---

## Learning goals
- Understand objectives of antitrust and sector regulation, and how enforcement agencies frame evidence.
- Map economic questions to legal elements (market definition, market power, competitive effects, remedies).
- Recognize when empirical, documentary, and expert evidence substitute or complement one another.

## Core narrative
- Brief history and institutions (DOJ/FTC, EU DG COMP, courts, regulators).
- Typical workflow from matter intake to report/testimony.
- How to scope data needs early: transactions, pricing, costs, contracts, internal documents.

::: {.callout-note title="Method box: Integrating empirics here"}
- Early descriptive stats and dashboards for screening.
- Screening metrics: concentration (HHI), shares, switching patterns, entry/exit.
- When to defer to qualitative evidence: thin data, nascent markets, highly idiosyncratic conduct.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Qualitative evidence"}
- Document review protocol: search terms, custodians, timelines.
- Interviewing fact witnesses and executives; mitigating hindsight bias.
- Summarizing evidence with chronologies and issue trees; linking to empirical priors.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Case box: Quick tour"}
- Microsoft (tying/exclusion), Visa/Mastercard (platforms), airline mergers (synergies vs. coordination), wage-fixing cases.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Comparative note"}
- Primary focus is US law and agency practice; comparative sidebars will flag EU (EC/DG COMP), UK (CMA), Japan (JFTC), China (SAMR), and other jurisdictions where standards or burdens diverge.
:::

## Code box: basic landscape plot
```{r}
#| label: orientation-hhi
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(fredr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
fredr_set_key(Sys.getenv("FRED_API_KEY"))

hhi <- fredr(series_id = "HHMSDODNS", observation_start = as.Date("2000-01-01"))
ggplot(hhi, aes(date, value)) +
  geom_line(color = "#0072B2") +
  labs(title = "US Bank Concentration (HHI)", x = NULL, y = "HHI") +
  theme_minimal()
```
